- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

22 23 --- 24 25 ## What is LayerInfinite? 26 27 LayerInfinite is an open-source decision layer that sits between your AI agent and your infrastructure. It intercepts every action your agent takes, records the outcome, and builds a real-time probability model of what works and what doesn't — per task type, per agent, across your entire fleet. 28 29 The next time your agent faces the same task, LayerInfinite routes it to the highest-probability action based on actual production data. Not benchmarks. Not embeddings. Real outcomes. 30 31 **The core problem it solves:** AI agents are stateless. Every session starts from zero. They retry failed actions, ignore production history, and have no mechanism to learn across deployments. LayerInfinite gives them persistent memory. 32 33 ### Who is it for? 34 35 LayerInfinite is designed for engineering teams building autonomous agents that take real-world actions. It works best with: 36 - **DevOps & Infrastructure Agents:** Resolving CI/CD failures, applying hotfixes, auto-reverting bad deployments. 37 - **Customer Support Agents:** Retrying failed API requests, switching payment providers, applying account fixes. 38 - **Data Engineering Agents:** Healing broken pipelines, auto-resolving schema drifts, failing over to replica databases. 39 40 ### Supported Frameworks 41 42 LayerInfinite is completely framework-agnostic. Because it instruments your Python or TypeScript functions directly using decorators, it works seamlessly alongside any agent framework: 43 - LangChain / LangGraph 44 - LlamaIndex 45 - AutoGen / AG2 46 - CrewAI 47 - Vercel AI SDK 48 - Custom-built agents in Plain Python / TypeScript 49 50 --- 51 52 ## Choose Your Starting Point 53 54 Your integration path depends on one question: **do you have existing agent logs?** 55 56 This is not a minor detail. Teams with production history get a fundamentally different — and dramatically more powerful — entry point than teams starting from scratch. 57 58 --- 59 60 ### Path A — You Have Existing Agent Logs 61 62 If your agent has been running in production — even if it was using LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, or a completely custom framework — you are not starting from zero. 63 64 ``` 65 1. Export logs from your current observability tool or database 66 2. Import them directly from the Dashboard — upload your log file 67 and LayerInfinite normalizes them automatically. 68 3. Open the Dashboard → Recommendations page 69 4. Identify the canonical task names and action names, now ranked 70 by success rate 71 5. Copy those exact names into your SDK integration 72 6. Deploy — your agent starts with a fully calibrated probability 73 model on the very first production call 74 ``` 75 76 > 🚨 **CRITICAL: The Golden Rule of Integration** 77 > 78 > **The names on the Recommendations page are canonical.** They are the keys the routing engine uses to map live SDK calls to historical outcomes. If you use different names in your code, you break the link — and your agent starts cold, as if it had no history at all. 79 > 80 > See the [Zero Cold-Start section](#️-the-zero-cold-start-advantage-historical-data-imports) for the full integration rule.

LayerInfinite

Decision intelligence infrastructure for autonomous AI agents.

Agents learn from production outcomes. No retraining. No vector databases. No guessing.

Dashboard · PyPI · npm · Benchmarks · Architecture · Contributing

--- ## What is LayerInfinite? LayerInfinite is an open-source decision layer that sits between your AI agent and your infrastructure. It intercepts every action your agent takes, records the outcome, and builds a real-time probability model of what works and what doesn't — per task type, per agent, across your entire fleet. The next time your agent faces the same task, LayerInfinite routes it to the highest-probability action based on actual production data. Not benchmarks. Not embeddings. Real outcomes. **The core problem it solves:** AI agents are stateless. Every session starts from zero. They retry failed actions, ignore production history, and have no mechanism to learn across deployments. LayerInfinite gives them persistent memory. ### Who is it for? LayerInfinite is designed for engineering teams building autonomous agents that take real-world actions. It works best with: - **DevOps & Infrastructure Agents:** Resolving CI/CD failures, applying hotfixes, auto-reverting bad deployments. - **Customer Support Agents:** Retrying failed API requests, switching payment providers, applying account fixes. - **Data Engineering Agents:** Healing broken pipelines, auto-resolving schema drifts, failing over to replica databases. ### Supported Frameworks LayerInfinite is completely framework-agnostic. Because it instruments your Python or TypeScript functions directly using decorators, it works seamlessly alongside any agent framework: - LangChain / LangGraph - LlamaIndex - AutoGen / AG2 - CrewAI - Vercel AI SDK - Custom-built agents in Plain Python / TypeScript --- ## Choose Your Starting Point Your integration path depends on one question: **do you have existing agent logs?** This is not a minor detail. Teams with production history get a fundamentally different — and dramatically more powerful — entry point than teams starting from scratch. --- ### Path A — You Have Existing Agent Logs If your agent has been running in production — even if it was using LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, or a completely custom framework — you are not starting from zero. ``` 1. Export logs from your current observability tool or database 2. Import them directly from the Dashboard — upload your log file and LayerInfinite normalizes them automatically. 3. Open the Dashboard → Recommendations page 4. Identify the canonical task names and action names, now ranked by success rate 5. Copy those exact names into your SDK integration 6. Deploy — your agent starts with a fully calibrated probability model on the very first production call ``` > 🚨 **CRITICAL: The Golden Rule of Integration** > > **The names on the Recommendations page are canonical.** They are the keys the routing engine uses to map live SDK calls to historical outcomes. If you use different names in your code, you break the link — and your agent starts cold, as if it had no history at all. > > See the [Zero Cold-Start section](#️-the-zero-cold-start-advantage-historical-data-imports) for the full integration rule.

1	# Architecture Deep Dive
2	
3	LayerInfinite is designed from the ground up for **durability**, **compliance**, and **sub-5ms decision latency**. It is fundamentally a deterministic, append-only system built on top of PostgreSQL, designed to sit synchronously in the hot-path of autonomous AI agents.
4	
5	## Core Philosophy
6	
7	1. **Deterministic over Probabilistic:** 
8	   LLMs are fundamentally non-deterministic. LayerInfinite brings determinism back to the execution layer. Actions are ranked by a deterministic mathematical formula (`success_count / total_count * recency_weight`), not by a vector similarity search or an LLM call.
9	2. **Auto-Instrumentation First:** 
10	   Developers should not have to write custom tracking telemetry. The SDK uses decorators (`@li.action`) to hook into execution contexts, automatically trapping `return` values and `Exceptions` to log outcomes.
11	3. **Immutability:** 
12	   No log is ever deleted or overwritten. All `fact_outcomes` are append-only. This is required for EU AI Act compliance and internal enterprise auditing.
13	
14	## System Components
15	
16	### 1. The SDK (Client)
17	The SDK runs alongside your agent (Python or JS/TS). It acts as an interceptor. 
18	- When an `@li.action` is invoked, the SDK records the `start_time`.
19	- When the function exits, the SDK immediately ships a `LogOutcomeRequest` to the backend asynchronously.
20	- The SDK utilizes **L1 Memory Caching** to ensure that rapid repeated requests for action routing do not hit the network.
21	
22	### 2. The Ingestion Engine (Hono Edge API)
23	The ingestion API is designed for immense throughput.
24	- **Strict Validation:** Uses Zod (TS) to ensure bad data never hits the database.
25	- **Durable Queue:** High-volume logging requests are pushed to a `dim_pending_signal_registrations` queue, allowing the system to decouple immediate ingestion from heavy analytical inserts.
26	
27	### 3. The Scoring Engine (Supabase / Postgres)
28	The heart of LayerInfinite is PostgreSQL.
29	Instead of querying raw logs on every request, LayerInfinite uses **Materialized Views** and **RPC Functions** to maintain a hot cache of probability scores. 
30	- `get_recommendations` executes a highly optimized SQL query that filters by `agent_id` and `issue_type`, returning ranked actions in under 5ms.
31	- Recency weighting ensures that if an API endpoint breaks today, the action's score decays instantly, routing the agent to a fallback action before it causes a major incident.
32	
33	## Security & Privacy
34	
35	**LayerInfinite does NOT store PII.**
36	When the SDK extracts context from a function call to log an outcome, it automatically scrubs parameter names matching common PII patterns (`email`, `password`, `ssn`, `api_key`). String values longer than 64 characters or values that look like bearer tokens are completely dropped.
37	
38	We store *metadata* about the decision (success boolean, latency, error codes), never the sensitive payload.

file.content:
# Architecture Deep Dive

LayerInfinite is designed from the ground up for **durability**, **compliance**, and **sub-5ms decision latency**. It is fundamentally a deterministic, append-only system built on top of PostgreSQL, designed to sit synchronously in the hot-path of autonomous AI agents.

## Core Philosophy

1. **Deterministic over Probabilistic:** 
   LLMs are fundamentally non-deterministic. LayerInfinite brings determinism back to the execution layer. Actions are ranked by a deterministic mathematical formula (`success_count / total_count * recency_weight`), not by a vector similarity search or an LLM call.
2. **Auto-Instrumentation First:** 
   Developers should not have to write custom tracking telemetry. The SDK uses decorators (`@li.action`) to hook into execution contexts, automatically trapping `return` values and `Exceptions` to log outcomes.
3. **Immutability:** 
   No log is ever deleted or overwritten. All `fact_outcomes` are append-only. This is required for EU AI Act compliance and internal enterprise auditing.

## System Components

### 1. The SDK (Client)
The SDK runs alongside your agent (Python or JS/TS). It acts as an interceptor. 
- When an `@li.action` is invoked, the SDK records the `start_time`.
- When the function exits, the SDK immediately ships a `LogOutcomeRequest` to the backend asynchronously.
- The SDK utilizes **L1 Memory Caching** to ensure that rapid repeated requests for action routing do not hit the network.

### 2. The Ingestion Engine (Hono Edge API)
The ingestion API is designed for immense throughput.
- **Strict Validation:** Uses Zod (TS) to ensure bad data never hits the database.
- **Durable Queue:** High-volume logging requests are pushed to a `dim_pending_signal_registrations` queue, allowing the system to decouple immediate ingestion from heavy analytical inserts.

### 3. The Scoring Engine (Supabase / Postgres)
The heart of LayerInfinite is PostgreSQL.
Instead of querying raw logs on every request, LayerInfinite uses **Materialized Views** and **RPC Functions** to maintain a hot cache of probability scores. 
- `get_recommendations` executes a highly optimized SQL query that filters by `agent_id` and `issue_type`, returning ranked actions in under 5ms.
- Recency weighting ensures that if an API endpoint breaks today, the action's score decays instantly, routing the agent to a fallback action before it causes a major incident.

## Security & Privacy

**LayerInfinite does NOT store PII.**
When the SDK extracts context from a function call to log an outcome, it automatically scrubs parameter names matching common PII patterns (`email`, `password`, `ssn`, `api_key`). String values longer than 64 characters or values that look like bearer tokens are completely dropped.

We store *metadata* about the decision (success boolean, latency, error codes), never the sensitive payload.

file.content:
---
name: Layerinfinite SDK — 4 fixes implemented and verified
description: Status of the 4 product-grade fixes to the Layerinfinite Python SDK v0.3.2
type: project
---

All 4 fixes implemented in the local SDK (`layer5/sdks/python/layerinfinite/`) and proof agent (`realworld_proof_agent.py`). User confirmed all four executed perfectly.

**Fix 1 — Exploration Floor:** `min_observations_per_action=20` param in `__init__`. `_build_execution_order` promotes least-observed action to front. Counts tracked in `_obs_counts` dict.

**Fix 2 — Data Resilience:** `normalize_business_outcome()` static method. Fuzzy action_name matching via `difflib.get_close_matches` in `log_outcome()`. Contradiction quarantine on `ingestion_quality.is_inconsistent`.

**Fix 3 — Trust UX:** `Suggestion` dataclass gains `outcomes_needed` + `cold_start` fields. Cold-start progress printed in `_fetch_scores`. Rich `LowConfidenceError` messages with gap/candidate/hint. `dispatch_auto` catches `LowConfidenceError` as `"li_abstained"` and logs outcome back to LI.

**Fix 4 — Auto Graduation:** `auto_graduate=False` param (opt-in). `_maybe_graduate()` maps trust_status → mode and switches `_mode` in place. Called after every log_outcome response (both async and public paths).

**Why:** User ran proof agent (`realworld_proof_agent.py`) across 200 baseline / 200 recommend / observe / 100 assist / 100 auto tickets. Identified exploration gap (payment_failed stuck at 38% confidence), data fragility, poor abstain UX, and no auto mode-switching.

**How to apply:** These fixes are in the local SDK only. If user asks about SDK behavior, these are the current capabilities. Zero-config SDK fix was planned but NOT implemented — deferred by user.

**Session from ~42 days ago** — 4 SDK fixes for the LayerInfinite Python SDK v0.3.2:

1. **Exploration Floor** — Added `min_observations_per_action=20` param to ensure least-observed actions get tried before confidence is trusted
2. **Data Resilience** — Added `normalize_business_outcome()` and fuzzy action-name matching to handle dirty production data
3. **Trust UX** — Added `outcomes_needed` / `cold_start` fields to `Suggestion`, richer `LowConfidenceError` messages, and `li_abstained` handling
4. **Auto Graduation** — Opt-in `auto_graduate=False` param that auto-switches mode from observe → assist → auto as trust thresholds are met

All 4 fixes were verified working with a proof agent across 600 test tickets. A planned "zero-config SDK" fix was deferred.

1	# Architecture Deep Dive
2	
3	LayerInfinite is designed from the ground up for **durability**, **compliance**, and **sub-5ms decision latency**. It is fundamentally a deterministic, append-only system built on top of PostgreSQL, designed to sit synchronously in the hot-path of autonomous AI agents.
4	
5	## Core Philosophy
6	
7	1. **Deterministic over Probabilistic:** 
8	   LLMs are fundamentally non-deterministic. LayerInfinite brings determinism back to the execution layer. Actions are ranked by a deterministic mathematical formula (`success_count / total_count * recency_weight`), not by a vector similarity search or an LLM call.
9	2. **Auto-Instrumentation First:** 
10	   Developers should not have to write custom tracking telemetry. The SDK uses decorators (`@li.action`) to hook into execution contexts, automatically trapping `return` values and `Exceptions` to log outcomes.
11	3. **Immutability:** 
12	   No log is ever deleted or overwritten. All `fact_outcomes` are append-only. This is required for EU AI Act compliance and internal enterprise auditing.
13	
14	## System Components
15	
16	### 1. The SDK (Client)
17	The SDK runs alongside your agent (Python or JS/TS). It acts as an interceptor. 
18	- When an `@li.action` is invoked, the SDK records the `start_time`.
19	- When the function exits, the SDK immediately ships a `LogOutcomeRequest` to the backend asynchronously.
20	- The SDK utilizes **L1 Memory Caching** to ensure that rapid repeated requests for action routing do not hit the network.
21	
22	### 2. The Ingestion Engine (Hono Edge API)
23	The ingestion API is designed for immense throughput.
24	- **Strict Validation:** Uses Zod (TS) to ensure bad data never hits the database.
25	- **Durable Queue:** High-volume logging requests are pushed to a `dim_pending_signal_registrations` queue, allowing the system to decouple immediate ingestion from heavy analytical inserts.
26	
27	### 3. The Scoring Engine (Supabase / Postgres)
28	The heart of LayerInfinite is PostgreSQL.
29	Instead of querying raw logs on every request, LayerInfinite uses **Materialized Views** and **RPC Functions** to maintain a hot cache of probability scores. 
30	- `get_recommendations` executes a highly optimized SQL query that filters by `agent_id` and `issue_type`, returning ranked actions in under 5ms.
31	- Recency weighting ensures that if an API endpoint breaks today, the action's score decays instantly, routing the agent to a fallback action before it causes a major incident.
32	
33	## Security & Privacy
34	
35	**LayerInfinite does NOT store PII.**
36	When the SDK extracts context from a function call to log an outcome, it automatically scrubs parameter names matching common PII patterns (`email`, `password`, `ssn`, `api_key`). String values longer than 64 characters or values that look like bearer tokens are completely dropped.
37	
38	We store *metadata* about the decision (success boolean, latency, error codes), never the sensitive payload.
39

file.content:
# Architecture Deep Dive

LayerInfinite is designed from the ground up for **durability**, **compliance**, and **sub-5ms decision latency**. It is fundamentally a deterministic, append-only system built on top of PostgreSQL, designed to sit synchronously in the hot-path of autonomous AI agents.

## Core Philosophy

1. **Deterministic over Probabilistic:** 
   LLMs are fundamentally non-deterministic. LayerInfinite brings determinism back to the execution layer. Actions are ranked by a deterministic mathematical formula (`success_count / total_count * recency_weight`), not by a vector similarity search or an LLM call.
2. **Auto-Instrumentation First:** 
   Developers should not have to write custom tracking telemetry. The SDK uses decorators (`@li.action`) to hook into execution contexts, automatically trapping `return` values and `Exceptions` to log outcomes.
3. **Immutability:** 
   No log is ever deleted or overwritten. All `fact_outcomes` are append-only. This is required for EU AI Act compliance and internal enterprise auditing.

## System Components

### 1. The SDK (Client)
The SDK runs alongside your agent (Python or JS/TS). It acts as an interceptor. 
- When an `@li.action` is invoked, the SDK records the `start_time`.
- When the function exits, the SDK immediately ships a `LogOutcomeRequest` to the backend asynchronously.
- The SDK utilizes **L1 Memory Caching** to ensure that rapid repeated requests for action routing do not hit the network.

### 2. The Ingestion Engine (Hono Edge API)
The ingestion API is designed for immense throughput.
- **Strict Validation:** Uses Zod (TS) to ensure bad data never hits the database.
- **Durable Queue:** High-volume logging requests are pushed to a `dim_pending_signal_registrations` queue, allowing the system to decouple immediate ingestion from heavy analytical inserts.

### 3. The Scoring Engine (Supabase / Postgres)
The heart of LayerInfinite is PostgreSQL.
Instead of querying raw logs on every request, LayerInfinite uses **Materialized Views** and **RPC Functions** to maintain a hot cache of probability scores. 
- `get_recommendations` executes a highly optimized SQL query that filters by `agent_id` and `issue_type`, returning ranked actions in under 5ms.
- Recency weighting ensures that if an API endpoint breaks today, the action's score decays instantly, routing the agent to a fallback action before it causes a major incident.

## Security & Privacy

**LayerInfinite does NOT store PII.**
When the SDK extracts context from a function call to log an outcome, it automatically scrubs parameter names matching common PII patterns (`email`, `password`, `ssn`, `api_key`). String values longer than 64 characters or values that look like bearer tokens are completely dropped.

We store *metadata* about the decision (success boolean, latency, error codes), never the sensitive payload.

48	| **Description** | Multi-tenant, append-only middleware between AI agents and enterprise systems. Provides outcome scoring, policy decisions, trust management, temporal analytics, simulation, and an MCP server for AI-agent integration. |

50	**Claimed vs Actual:** The PROJECT_REPORT.md describes a "Production-Ready v3.2.0" system with 10 layers and sub-5ms query latency. In reality, the codebase is extensive and well-structured but shows no evidence of production deployment (no SLOs, no load-test results, no real-world traffic). Many features are implemented but untested at scale.

121	| 4 | Adaptive Policy Engine | Complete | 7-rule explore/exploit/escalate decision tree |
122	| 5 | Temporal Trending + Gap Detection | Complete | Context drift, silent failure, trend labels |
123	| 6 | Trust + Suspension Lifecycle | Complete | Exponential smoothing, 4 trust statuses |

298

301	| 001–029 | 29 | Core schema | Dimensions, fact_outcomes, episodes, MVs, RLS, trust, events, indexes, cron, vector, auth, scoring, decisions, sequences, counterfactuals, world_model, notification, idempotency |
302	| 031–067 | 37 | Enhancements + Fixes | Verifier, validation mode, sandbox, backprop, rate limiting, backfills, object fixes, matview cron, data integrity, seed removal, trust snapshots, embedding versioning, drift, mv episode patterns rewrite, trust audit fix, mv confidence floor, refresh scores RPC |
303	| 071–131 | 61 | Maturity + Stability | Task grouping, pipeline hardening, context vector fixes, 500 error fixes, institutional knowledge seed, signal contracts, discrepancy log, tenant merge, mv tap fixes, unique constraints, customer_id scoping, sequence tenant invariants, cohort cycles, ingestion quality, scoring tiers, cross-event taxonomy, task performance MV, import jobs, inference, action aliases, backfills, clustering, step performance, context-aware MVs, dashboard indexes, ingress queue, retention policy, waitlist, debug, pgcrypto fix, trigger path fix |

305	### 4.3 Core Schema
306	
307	**dim_customers**
308	- `customer_id UUID PK`, `company_name`, `industry`, `tier`, `api_key_hash`, `pruning_config JSONB`, `config JSONB`
309	- RLS: service_role only
310	
311	**dim_agents**
312	- `agent_id UUID PK`, `customer_id FK`, `agent_type`, `llm_model`, `tools_available TEXT[]`
313	- RLS: scoped by customer
314	
315	**fact_outcomes** (the central table — append-only)
316	- `outcome_id UUID PK`, `customer_id FK`, `agent_id FK`, `action_id FK`, `context_id FK`
317	- `success BOOLEAN`, `outcome_score NUMERIC`, `composite_score NUMERIC`
318	- `confidence NUMERIC`, `trend_delta NUMERIC`, `raw_context TEXT`
319	- `episode_id UUID`, `decision_id UUID`, `idempotency_key TEXT UNIQUE`
320	- `timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ`, `ingestion_source TEXT`, `task TEXT`, `resource_cost NUMERIC`
321	- **BEFORE UPDATE trigger raises EXCEPTION** — truly append-only
322	- Indexes: (customer_id, context_id, timestamp DESC), (agent_id, timestamp DESC), (customer_id, task, agent_id, source)
323	
324	**mv_action_scores** (materialized view, refreshed ~5 min)
325	- Pre-aggregates per (customer_id, context_id, action_id): `weighted_success_rate`, `confidence`, `trend_delta`, `total_attempts`, `avg_response_ms`, `last_outcome_at`, `view_refreshed_at`
326	
327	**agent_trust_scores**
328	- `agent_id PK`, `trust_score NUMERIC`, `trust_status TEXT` (trusted/probation/sandbox/suspended/new/degraded)
329	- `consecutive_failures INT`, `total_decisions INT`, `correct_decisions INT`
330	
331	### 4.4 Trust Score Lifecycle
332	
333	```
334	Cold start → trust_score = 0.70 (initial prior)
335	    │
336	    ├── On failure: score × 0.9^n (n = consecutive failures)
337	    │   └── 5 failures → 'degraded'
338	    │   └── 10 failures → 'suspended'
339	    │
340	    ├── On success: score × 1.03
341	    │   └── score ≥ 0.6 → 'trusted'
342	    │   └── score 0.3–0.6 → 'probation'
343	    │
344	    └── Coordinated failure detection: 3+ agents failing same action
345	        in 5 minutes → freeze trust (infrastructure attribution)
346	```
347	
348	### 4.5 Key Constraints & Invariants
349	
350	- `fact_outcomes`: UPDATE trigger raises EXCEPTION (append-only enforcement)
351	- `schema_migrations`: version tracked, rollback supported
352	- `fact_outcomes`: idempotency_key unique constraint prevents duplicates
353	- Rate limit: DB-backed via `consume_rate_limit_bucket` RPC with fail-open behavior (SECURITY ISSUE)
354	- RLS on all tenant-scoped tables (though some have `authenticated = TRUE` — SECURITY ISSUE)

408	
409	### 5.4 The Simulation Engine (3-Tier)
410	
411	| Tier | File | Technique | Latency | Purpose |
412	|------|------|-----------|---------|---------|
413	| Tier 1 | `tier1.ts` | Wilson Score Interval + bootstrap | <10ms | Fast estimate for <5 samples per action |
414	| Tier 2 | `tier2.ts` | LightGBM quantile regression (q10, q50, q90) | <100ms | Learned outcome prediction for 5–200 samples |
415	| Tier 3 | `tier3-mcts.ts` | MCTS with UCT: 500 sims, MAX_DEPTH=5, 8s timeout | ~7,500ms | Deep search for >200 samples or high-stakes |
416	
417	**World Model** (`world-model.ts`):
418	- LightGBM tree evaluation: 600 evaluations per prediction (200 trees × 3 quantiles)
419	- 30-min cache per (context_hash, action_hash)
420	- Canary routing with configurable traffic percentage (`canary_traffic_pct`)
421	- 4 validation gates before model activation (R² ≥ 0.20, RMSE ≤ 0.35, CI coverage ≥ 85%, interval width ≤ 0.60)
422	
423	### 5.5 The Scoring Formula
424	
425	```
426	composite_score = (
427	    0.40 × success_rate   +  // Bayesian smoothed: (successes + 1) / (total + 2)
428	    0.20 × confidence     +  // Wilson-style: n/(n+10)
429	    0.20 × trend          +  // Normalized trend_delta to [0,1]
430	    0.10 × salience       +  // Action importance (currently always 1.0)
431	    0.10 × recency           // Decay over 7 days: max(0, 1 - age_hours/168)
432	) × context_match            // Cosine similarity to context vector
433	```
434	
435	**Cold Start Fallback (3 tiers):**
436	1. Customer-specific `mv_action_scores` data
437	2. Context-specific `dim_institutional_knowledge` priors
438	3. Global fallback `dim_institutional_knowledge WHERE context_type = 'global'`
439	
440	### 5.6 Policy Engine Decision Tree
441	
442	```
443	1. Suspended agent            → ESCALATE
444	2. New agent (no history)     → EXPLORE (lowest-sample action)
445	3. Degraded agent             → SANDBOX (human review required)
446	4. Sandbox agent              → SANDBOX
447	5. Cold start / low confidence → EXPLORE (lowest-sample action)
448	6. Conservative + score > 0.8 → EXPLOIT
449	7. High score + high confidence → epsilon-greedy
450	8. Medium score (0.5–0.85)    → confidence-weighted exploit/explore
451	9. All scores < escalation    → ESCALATE
452	10. Default                   → EXPLORE (2nd action)
453	```
454	
455	### 5.7 Most Critical Route: `POST /v1/log-outcome`
456	
457	This is the most complex route in the system (1,603 lines — the single largest file).
458	
459	**Middleware chain:** `primaryAuth` → `rateLimitMiddleware` → `validateActionMiddleware`
460	
461	**Handler pipeline:**
462	1. Zod validation (30+ fields)
463	2. PII scrubbing via `sanitize.ts`
464	3. Task inference (`inferTask()` — maps issue_type to canonical task name)
465	4. Score inference (if `outcome_score` absent)
466	5. Decision linkage (lookup `fact_decisions` by `decision_id`)
467	6. Data quality computation
468	7. Semantic cluster inference
469	8. Durable queue enqueue (in production) or direct insert
470	
471	**Post-outcome orchestration** (via `Promise.allSettled`):
472	- Trust update (exponential smoothing + coordinated failure check)
473	- Context drift detection
474	- Silent failure detection
475	- Counterfactual writing
476	- Sequence tracking + reward backpropagation
477	- Latency spike detection
478	- Score cache invalidation
479	- Predictive drift analysis

526	## 8. Machine Learning Training Pipeline
527	
528	**Location:** `layer5/training/`

530	**Pipeline:**
531	```
532	features.py → train_world_model.py → validate_model.py → export_model.py

538	
539	**Model:** LightGBM quantile regression (3 quantiles: q10, q50, q90)
540	- 200 trees per quantile model
541	- 10 input features: action_id, episode_position, prev_actions ×3, context_frequency, time features (hour, day, month, weekday)
542	- Training frequency: weekly cron (via Cloud Run Job)

544	**Validation Gates** (all must pass for model activation):
545	- R² ≥ 0.20 (explains at least 20% of outcome variance)
546	- RMSE ≤ 0.35 (on 0–1 scale)
547	- 95% CI coverage ≥ 0.85
548	- Average interval width ≤ 0.60

605

618	  ├── src/param-resolver.ts← 3-layer parameter resolution
619	  ├── src/config.ts        ← Env validation + config freeze

628	| Tool | Bootstrap | Recommend | Assist | Auto |

631	| `li_observe` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

640	
641	### 11.3 4 Modes
642	
643	Controlled by `LAYERINFINITE_MODE` env var:
644	- **(absent) = Bootstrap** — log/observe only. LI learns from outcomes.
645	- **`recommend`** — LI injects scored recommendations via `layerinfinite://tasks/{task}` resource.
646	- **`assist`** — `li_action` warns if a better action exists; agent decides.
647	- **`auto`** — `li_action` redirects to proven best action (needs ≥90% confidence). Falls back to assist.
648	
649	### 11.4 Key Implementation Details
650	
651	- **RestClient:** Exponential backoff retries, timeout handling, structured error responses
652	- **EpisodeTracker:** In-memory Set with 30-min TTL expiry — prevents redirect loops
653	- **ParamResolver:** 3-layer resolution (carry-over → defaults → required params) for auto-mode redirects
654	- **Logger:** Structured JSON to stderr with levels and tool scoping
655	- **Config:** Frozen config object after env validation — fails fast on missing API key
656	- **Graceful shutdown:** SIGINT/SIGTERM handling with debounce
657	- **Pre-compiled:** `dist/` directory ready to run — no build step needed
658

677	## 12. Data Flows (End-to-End Walkthrough)
678	
679	### 12.1 The 5 Stages (from li_architecture_walkthrough.md)
680	
681	```
682	① Historical Import (bootstrap)   → fact_outcomes
683	② Live Outcome Logging (MCP)      → fact_outcomes
684	③ Backend Orchestration (8 tasks) → trust, drift, sequences, counterfactuals
685	④ Scoring Engine (6-factor)       → mv_action_scores → composite_score
686	⑤ Recommendation Engine (gates)   → ranked_actions → li_action response
687	```
688	
689	### 12.2 Complete MCP Flow
690	
691	```
692	User: "Customer #12345 has a payment failure, handle it"
693	
694	LLM: li_action(task="payment_failed", context={...})
695	  └─ MCP Server
696	       ├─ GET /v1/get-scores (parallel)
697	       │    ├─ Auth → agent_id, customer_id
698	       │    ├─ Rate limit check
699	       │    ├─ Context resolution → context_id
700	       │    ├─ Scoring → 6-factor composite scores
701	       │    ├─ Policy → trust check + customer config
702	       │    └─ Decision → bufferDecision() → decision_id
703	       └─ GET /v1/recommendations (parallel)
704	            ├─ Task performance data
705	            ├─ Noise gate check
706	            └─ Shadow signal blending
707	
708	LLM executes action (retry_payment) → GATEWAY_TIMEOUT (failed)
709	
710	LLM: li_log(decision_id, action="retry_payment", success=false)
711	  └─ MCP Server → POST /v1/log-outcome
712	       ├─ Auth + rate limit + validateAction
713	       ├─ Zod validation + PII scrubbing
714	       ├─ Task inference + Score inference
715	       ├─ Decision linkage
716	       ├─ enqueueDurable() → queue → ingestOutcome() → fact_outcomes
717	       └─ orchestrateOutcome():
718	            8 parallel tasks via Promise.allSettled()
719	
720	LLM: li_fallback(task="payment_failed", failed_action="retry_payment")
721	  └─ MCP Server → GET /v1/get-scores (fresh, cache invalidated)
722	       └─ Filters out retry_payment → returns switch_provider
723	
724	LLM executes switch_provider → SUCCESS → li_log
725	  └─ Learning loop closed: switch_provider ranked higher next time
726	```
727

741	| `layer5/sdks/python/tests/` | Pytest | 16 | Interceptor, pipeline, client, integrations |
742	| **Total** | | **~340** | |
743	
744	### 13.2 Test Gaps
745	
746	| Area | Status | Risk |
747	|------|--------|------|
748	| Dashboard tests | ❌ None | UI regressions undetectable |
749	| E2E tests | ❌ None | Full pipeline failures silent |
750	| Performance benchmarks | ❌ None | Latency regressions invisible |
751	| Chaos/resilience tests | ❌ None | Failure modes untested |
752	| Migration rollback tests | ❌ None | Schema downgrade untested |
753	| Load tests | ❌ None | Scaling characteristics unknown |
754	| CI test gates | ⚠️ Pending | Workflows exist but not fully enforced |
755	
756	---
757	
758	## 14. CI/CD
759	
760	**4 workflows** in `.github/workflows/`:
761	
762	| Workflow | Trigger | Purpose |
763	|----------|---------|---------|
764	| `ci.yml` | PRs + push to main | TypeScript typecheck, Python typecheck, tests |
765	| `production-readiness-checks.yml` | PRs + push to main | Production readiness validation |
766	| `publish-npm-sdk.yml` | Tag `sdk-v*` | Build + publish TypeScript SDK to npm |
767	| `publish-python-sdk.yml` | Tag `sdk-v*` | Build + publish Python SDK to PyPI |
768	
769	**Notable:** More complete than initially reported — CI workflow exists. However:
770	- No coverage reporting in CI
771	- No migration smoke tests
772	- No dashboard typecheck
773	- Benchmark suite (`benchmark/`) exists but not wired into CI
774	
775	---
776	
777	## 15. Agent Analysis Findings
778	
779	### 15.1 Research Quality Audit
780	
781	**Documents Analyzed:** architecture_designs.md, architecture_research.md, research.md, PROJECT_REPORT.md, implementation_plan_MCP, li_architecture_walkthrough.md
782	
783	**Key Findings:**
784	
785	1. **Document quality scores (1-10):**
786	   - architecture_research.md: **8/10** — Strongest document. Real academic citations, explicit pattern mappings, honest risk table.
787	   - implementation_plan_MCP: **7/10** — Good lifecycle-driven design, honest self-critique of v4 errors. MCP plan is now realized as built code.
788	   - research.md: **7/10** — Specific platform comparisons, actionable code snippets.
789	   - architecture_designs.md: **5/10** — Well-structured but describes current state inaccurately vs codebase.
790	   - PROJECT_REPORT.md: **6/10** — Concrete artifact counts verifiable, but "production-ready" claims unsubstantiated.
791	   - li_architecture_walkthrough.md: **6/10** — Detailed flow but no load-test evidence for concurrent execution claims.
792	
793	2. **Critical gap: No latency budget analysis.**
794	   None of the 6 documents provide end-to-end latency measurements for any API endpoint.
795	
796	3. **Critical gap: No cost model.**
797	   No document addresses operational costs: Supabase pricing at scale, per-outcome storage cost, SDK runtime overhead.
798	
799	4. **No adversarial robustness analysis.**
800	   No discussion of prompt injection against MCP server, data poisoning of scoring engine, or gradient inversion.
801	
802	5. **Sources are aging.** 5 of 12 cited academic references are from 2018 or earlier. Missing: modern agent tool-use literature (ToolLLM, Gorilla), deep bandit literature, GRPO (2024).
803	
804	6. **Bootstrap-then-intelligence lifecycle is the best design decision.**
805	   The MCP plan's insight — new users only see `li_log`, and `li_action`/decide only appears when sufficient data exists — is elegant and correct. Now fully implemented in the MCP server.
806	
807	### 15.2 Architectural Evaluation
808	
809	**What was audited:** Existing lib modules (scoring.ts, policy-engine.ts, outcome-orchestrator.ts, simulation/*, drift-detector.ts, ips-engine.ts, reward-backprop.ts, context-embed.ts, verifier.ts, sanitize.ts, schema-inferrer.ts, adapters/)
810	
811	**Key Findings:**
812	
813	1. **55% of a Proactive Guardian (Arch D) already exists.**
814	   - Detection infrastructure: silent failure, context drift, coordinated failure, predictive drift — all built and running.
815	   - Trust system: exponential smoothing, status lifecycle, atomic audit RPC — complete.
816	   - SDK interceptor layer: fetch/pg/child_process patching — deployed in both TS and Python.
817	   - Policy engine: pure-function decision tree with zero DB calls — exact match for guardian intervention logic.
818	   - **Gap:** only post-hoc detection exists. No preemptive interception.
819	
820	2. **60% of the RL Simulation Engine (Arch B) already exists.**
821	   - World model: LightGBM quantile regression, canary routing, 30-min cache — built and production-ready.
822	   - MCTS: full UCT implementation with 500 simulations, 8s timeout, MAX_DEPTH=5 — built and tested.
823	   - Counterfactuals: IPS-weighting, reward backpropagation with γ=0.85 — built.
824	   - **Gap:** No online policy gradient update loop. MCTS not wired into live recommendation path.
825	
826	3. **The existing simulation engine is the most under-leveraged asset.**
827	   - `tier3-mcts.ts` has full MCTS with UCT, SELECT/EXPAND/ROLLOUT/BACKPROPAGATE cycle.
828	   - But MCTS does not feed into live recommendation responses — standalone simulation only.
829	
830	4. **score cache is warming properly.**
831	   - LRU Map with 1000-entry eviction, 5-min TTL.
832	   - `invalidateCache()` called after each outcome write.
833	
834	### 15.3 Technical Feasibility
835	
836	**Key Findings:**
837	
838	1. **MCTS is NOT real-time without ONNX export.** Current TypeScript tree-walking does 600 evaluations per prediction at ~15ms each. 500 MCTS simulations = ~7,500ms. For real-time use (<100ms), ONNX/WASM export or simulation count reduction is required.
839	
840	2. **The 10-feature world model is too sparse.** Encodes action ID, episode position, 3 previous actions, context frequency, and 4 cyclical time features. Captures nothing about actual reasoning content, tool output semantics, or environment state.
841	
842	3. **Agent integration model matters.** Current MCP interception (tools/call only) cannot do preemptive blocking. Architecture D needs framework-level hooks.
843	
844	4. **Critical dependency: LLM provider choice must be locked before any architecture work.** Architectures requiring LLM calls need a provider decision: Anthropic (500-3000ms), OpenAI (200-1000ms), or local (50-500ms).
845	
846	### 15.4 Testing & Validation
847	
848	**Key Findings:**
849	
850	1. **~515 new tests across 69 new files needed** to properly validate the architecture upgrade.
851	
852	2. **World model validation thresholds are dangerously low:**
853	   - R² ≥ 0.20: 80% of outcome variance is unexplained
854	   - RMSE ≤ 0.35: average prediction error is 35% of full range
855	
856	3. **CI/CD must be built before any architecture changes.** Currently no pre-merge test gates enforced.
857	
858	### 15.5 Quality Assurance Review
859	
860	**Overall Readiness Score: 5/10**
861	
862	**Critical Issues Found:**
863	
864	1. **THE CURRENT STATE IS DESCRIBED THREE DIFFERENT WAYS ACROSS DOCUMENTS.**
865	   - `architecture_designs.md`: "passive MCP Interception Model" with 6 limitations
866	   - `PROJECT_REPORT.md`: "v3.2.0 production-ready" with 10 layers
867	   - `layer5/README.md`: "6-layer middleware"
868	   - **These cannot all be true.** Must reconcile before any new architecture work.
869	
870	2. **Implementation timelines are off by factor of 6-10x.**
871	   - `implementation_plan.md` proposes delivering 7 architectural pillars + multi-agent coordination in 6 weeks with 2-3 engineers. This is 6-9 months.
872	
873	3. **No "simplest thing that works" analysis exists.**
874	   - Before proposing MCTS with transformer world models, the documents skip past: better prompts, 100 regex rules as guardrails, human-in-the-loop for high-stakes decisions.
875	
876	### 15.6 Security Analysis
877	
878	**CRITICAL VULNERABILITIES FOUND:**
879	
880	#### 🔴 Critical
881	
882	1. **Webhook endpoint is completely unauthenticated.**
883	   - `POST /v1/webhook/:provider` has NO authentication middleware.
884	
885	2. **RLS policies leak cross-tenant data.**
886	   - `dim_contexts`: Migration 006 sets `FOR SELECT TO authenticated USING (TRUE)` — any authenticated dashboard user reads ALL customers' contexts.
887	   - `dim_actions`: Same — action names like `resolve_stripe_dispute` reveal business models.
888	   - `dim_institutional_knowledge`: Globally readable — contains cross-customer success rates.
889	   - `world_model_artifacts`: Globally readable.
890	
891	3. **`verifier.ts` has a silent no-op.**
892	   - `downstream_webhook` is a valid `VerifierSignal.source` but has NO handler. Webhook verification silently falls through.
893	
894	4. **Rate limiting fails open on DB error.**
895	   - If `consume_rate_limit_bucket` RPC fails, request is ALLOWED with no rate check.
896	
897	#### 🟡 High
898	
899	5. **`/internal/refresh-score-cache` uses non-timing-safe comparison.**
900	6. **`/health/deep` exposes system internals** (table names, schema version).
901	7. **Admin routes use same API key flow as agent routes.**
902	8. **Secrets may leak through raw_context** — no automatic secret redaction.
903	9. **Auth cache retains revoked keys for up to 5 minutes.**
904	
905	#### 🟢 Medium
906	
907	10. **`dim_institutional_knowledge` cross-customer sharing with no opt-in.**
908	11. **`policy-engine.ts` accepts unbounded customer config** — no schema constraints.
909	12. **The "auto" mode has no enforcement mechanism.** MCP tool description tells the LLM "You MUST execute the specified action" but there is NO middleware guard. Auto mode is a suggestion dressed as an instruction.
910	
911	---
912	
913	## 16. Known Issues & Vulnerabilities
914	
915	### Security (Must Fix Before Deployment)
916	
917	| ID | Issue | Severity | File/Route | Fix |
918	|----|-------|----------|------------|-----|
919	| S-1 | Webhook endpoint unauthenticated | 🔴 Critical | `routes/webhook.ts` | Add source-specific signature verification |
920	| S-2 | RLS: dim_contexts reads all tenants | 🔴 Critical | Migration 006 | Scope SELECT by customer |
921	| S-3 | RLS: dim_actions reads all tenants | 🔴 Critical | Migration 006 | Scope SELECT by customer |
922	| S-4 | RLS: dim_institutional_knowledge global read | 🔴 Critical | Migration related | Make tenant-scoped |
923	| S-5 | RLS: world_model_artifacts global read | 🔴 Critical | Migration related | Make tenant-scoped |
924	| S-6 | verifier.ts: downstream_webhook no-op | 🔴 Critical | `lib/verifier.ts:10` | Implement handler or remove enum value |
925	| S-7 | Rate limit fails open on DB error | 🔴 Critical | `middleware/rate-limit.ts` | Add circuit breaker with in-memory fallback |
926	| S-8 | Internal secret comparison not timing-safe | 🟡 High | `api/index.ts` | Use `crypto.timingSafeEqual()` |
927	| S-9 | Health/deep exposes system internals | 🟡 Medium | `api/index.ts` | Add auth to deep health |
928	| S-10 | Auth cache retains revoked keys 5 min | 🟡 Medium | `middleware/auth.ts` | Reduce cleanup interval |
929	| S-11 | Raw_context may contain secrets | 🟡 High | `routes/log-outcome.ts` | Add automatic secret regex redaction |
930	| S-12 | Admin routes share API key auth with agent routes | 🟡 Medium | `middleware/admin-auth.ts` | Separate admin key flow |
931	| S-13 | Policy config unbounded | 🟡 Medium | `lib/policy-engine.ts` | Add schema constraints per tier |
932	| S-14 | Rate limit X-Forwarded-For spoofable | 🟡 Medium | `middleware/rate-limit.ts` | Return 401 for unauthenticated |
933	| S-15 | No Content-Type enforcement on JSON routes | 🟡 Low | All routes | Validate Content-Type header |
934	
935	### Functional Issues
936	
937	| ID | Issue | Severity | Location |
938	|----|-------|----------|----------|
939	| F-1 | tier1.ts missing customer_id isolation on sequence tables | High | `simulation/tier1.ts` |
940	| F-2 | ips-engine.ts IPS_WEIGHT_CAP=0.3 still active (comment says removed) | Medium | `lib/ips-engine.ts` |
941	| F-3 | context-embed.ts O(n) linear scan instead of pgvector HNSW index | High | `lib/context-embed.ts:181-227` |
942	| F-4 | tier3-mcts.ts non-deterministic via Math.random() | Medium | `simulation/tier3-mcts.ts` |
943	| F-5 | world-model.ts + features.py feature sync enforced by comment only | High | `lib/simulation/world-model.ts` + `training/features.py` |
944	| F-6 | scoring.ts returns scalar composite_score — no confidence intervals | High | `lib/scoring.ts:119-131` |
945	| F-7 | outcome-orchestrator.ts Promise.allSettled incompatible with RL sync loops | Medium | `lib/outcome-orchestrator.ts` |
946	| F-8 | 50-outcome bootstrap threshold is arbitrary | Low | MCP server spec |
947	| F-9 | No fresh outcome ingestion for >4h — no recovery path for stale data | Medium | System-wide |
948	| F-10 | WebSocket/SSE not used — dashboard is polling-only | Low | Dashboard |
949	
950	### Testing & Quality Issues
951	
952	| ID | Issue | Severity |
953	|----|-------|----------|
954	| T-1 | No CI workflow enforced on PR/main | 🔴 Critical |
955	| T-2 | Dashboard has zero tests | 🟡 High |
956	| T-3 | No E2E tests | 🟡 High |
957	| T-4 | No performance benchmarks wired to CI | 🟡 High |
958	| T-5 | No load testing | 🟡 High |
959	| T-6 | No migration rollback tests | 🟡 Medium |
960	| T-7 | World model validation thresholds too low (R² ≥ 0.20) | 🟡 High |
961	| T-8 | 3 documents describe 3 different current states | 🔴 Critical |
962	
963	---
964	
965	## 17. Code Smells & Technical Debt
966	
967	### Architecture-Level
968	
969	1. **Promise.allSettled with fire-and-forget semantics.** 8 parallel tasks with no ordering guarantees. Incompatible with RL-style synchronous reward backpropagation.
970	
971	2. **Three-tier fallback for cold start is elegant but untested at scale.** Never validated with realistic data distributions.
972	
973	3. **5-factor scoring formula with hand-tuned weights.** Weights (0.40, 0.20, 0.20, 0.10, 0.10) are hardcoded constants, not learned from data.
974	
975	4. **Materialized view refresh cadence is a single timer.** No incremental refresh, no staleness budget.
976	
977	### Code-Level
978	
979	5. **Scoring types lack confidence intervals.** `ScoredAction.composite_score: number` is a scalar. Changing to include confidence bounds breaks every caller (12+ files).
980	
981	6. **Policy engine is synchronous only.** Cannot handle async LLM calls. Adding async requires splitting decision paths.
982	
983	7. **Feature engineering sync is a comment.** `world-model.ts` + `features.py` use a comment "Feature order MUST match" — no automated validation.
984	
985	8. **Customer ID isolation gap in simulation.** `tier1.ts` explicitly documents missing `customer_id` filter — known cross-tenant data leak.
986	
987	9. **MCTS is non-deterministic.** `Math.random()` used for UCB exploration. `randomFn` should be injectable.
988	
989	10. **MCP auto mode has no server-side enforcement.** The `li_action` tool's description says "You MUST execute the specified action" but the LLM can ignore it. No middleware guard exists.
990	
991	---
992	
993	*End of document. This captures the LayerInfinite v3.2.0 codebase as it exists in the real repository at `C:\Users\kusta\Layerinfinite\Outcome`, plus all findings from the 7-agent deep analysis. New architecture proposals (A–E) are documented separately in `architecture_designs.md`.*
994

# LayerInfinite — Project Architecture (v3.2.0 As-Is)

**Generated:** May 18, 2026 (rewritten against correct repo)
**Source:** `C:\Users\kusta\Layerinfinite\Outcome` — Repository audit + 7-agent deep analysis

> This documents ONLY the current (as-built) system — the v3.2.0 codebase.
> New architecture proposals (A–E from architecture_designs.md) are intentionally excluded.

---

## Table of Contents

1. [Project Identity & Status](#1-project-identity--status)
2. [Repository Topology](#2-repository-topology)
3. [System Architecture Overview](#3-system-architecture-overview)
4. [Database Layer (133 SQL Files)](#4-database-layer-133-sql-files)
5. [REST API (Hono + TypeScript)](#5-rest-api-hono--typescript)
6. [Dashboard (React + Vite)](#6-dashboard-react--vite)
7. [Supabase Edge Functions](#7-supabase-edge-functions)
8. [Machine Learning Training Pipeline](#8-machine-learning-training-pipeline)
9. [Instrumentation SDKs](#9-instrumentation-sdks)
10. [No-Code Connectors](#10-no-code-connectors)
11. [MCP Server (Built — Production-Ready)](#11-mcp-server-built--production-ready)
12. [Data Flows (End-to-End Walkthrough)](#12-data-flows-end-to-end-walkthrough)
13. [Current Testing Landscape](#13-current-testing-landscape)
14. [CI/CD](#14-cicd)
15. [Agent Analysis Findings](#15-agent-analysis-findings)
    - 15.1 Research Quality Audit
    - 15.2 Architectural Evaluation
    - 15.3 Technical Feasibility
    - 15.4 Testing & Validation
    - 15.5 Quality Assurance Review
    - 15.6 Security Analysis
16. [Known Issues & Vulnerabilities](#16-known-issues--vulnerabilities)
17. [Code Smells & Technical Debt](#17-code-smells--technical-debt)

---

## 1. Project Identity & Status

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Name** | LayerInfinite |
| **Tagline** | The world's first Outcome-Ranked Decision Intelligence Layer for Enterprise AI Agents |
| **Version** | 3.2.0 |
| **Status** | Active development (pre-production) |
| **Stack** | Hono (TypeScript) + PostgreSQL/Supabase + React + Python ML + MCP |
| **Description** | Multi-tenant, append-only middleware between AI agents and enterprise systems. Provides outcome scoring, policy decisions, trust management, temporal analytics, simulation, and an MCP server for AI-agent integration. |

**Claimed vs Actual:** The PROJECT_REPORT.md describes a "Production-Ready v3.2.0" system with 10 layers and sub-5ms query latency. In reality, the codebase is extensive and well-structured but shows no evidence of production deployment (no SLOs, no load-test results, no real-world traffic). Many features are implemented but untested at scale.

---

## 2. Repository Topology

```
C:\Users\kusta\Layerinfinite\Outcome\
├── .github/workflows/           # 4 CI/CD workflows
├── .vscode/                     # VS Code config
├── .vercel/                     # Vercel deployment config
├── benchmark/                   # Performance benchmarking suite
│   ├── runner.ts, li-client.ts, llm-client.ts, sudoku-engine.ts
│   └── benchmark_results_*.json
├── landing/                     # Static marketing landing page
├── layer4/                      # Legacy dashboard shell
│   └── dashboard/src/
│
├── layer5/                      # MAIN CODEBASE
│   ├── api/                     # Hono REST API (with dist/, routes/, lib/, middleware/, types/, workers/, tests/)
│   ├── dashboard/               # React + Vite admin dashboard
│   ├── supabase/                # Database + Edge Functions
│   │   ├── migrations/          # 127 SQL migrations (001–131, gaps at 030, 068-070)
│   │   ├── functions/           # 6 Deno Edge Functions
│   │   ├── seed/                # cold_start_priors.sql
│   │   └── config.toml
│   ├── sdks/                    # Instrumentation SDKs
│   │   ├── typescript/          # @layerinfinite/sdk-core
│   │   ├── python/              # layerinfinite-l5
│   │   └── smoke-test.js
│   ├── training/                # ML training pipeline (LightGBM quantile regression)
│   ├── tests/                   # 34 test files across layers 1–8 + auth
│   └── scripts/                 # verify-pgcron.sql, run-backfill-104.sql
│
├── packages/
│   └── mcp-server/              # @layerinfinite/mcp-server v1.0.0 (fully built, pre-compiled)
│       ├── src/                 # 14 source files (index.ts, 10 tools, resource, prompt, config, etc.)
│       ├── bin/cli.ts           # stdio transport entry point
│       ├── __tests__/           # 4 Vitest test files (557 test lines)
│       ├── examples/            # Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline config examples
│       └── dist/                # Pre-compiled JavaScript (ready to run)
│
├── ARCHITECTURE.md              # System architecture doc
├── architecture_designs.md      # Alternative architecture proposals (A–E)
├── architecture_research.md     # Decision optimization research
├── implementation_plan.md       # 6-week plan (trading-focused)
├── implementation_plan_MCP      # MCP server spec (original design doc)
├── RELEASE_NOTES_v0.3.1.md      # Release notes
├── AUTH_SETUP.md                # Authentication setup guide
├── research.md                  # Trading platforms research
├── railway.toml                 # Railway deployment config
├── README.md                    # Project README
├── package.json                 # Root config
└── .env / .env.local            # Environment config
```

**Total SQL files:** 133 (127 migrations + 6 non-migration: seed `cold_start_priors.sql`, scripts `run-backfill-104.sql` + `verify-pgcron.sql`, test gates `layer1/test_gate.sql`–`layer3/test_gate.sql`)

---

## 3. System Architecture Overview

### 3.1 Ten-Layer Model (as claimed)

PROJECT_REPORT.md describes 10 core phases/layers:

| Phase | Layer | Status | Description |
|-------|-------|--------|-------------|
| 1 | Structured Experience Memory | Complete | Star-schema with append-only `fact_outcomes` |
| 2 | Auth + Multi-Tenant | Complete | RLS isolation, API key hashing |
| 3 | Composite Scoring | Complete | 5-factor (later 6-factor) weighted formula |
| 4 | Adaptive Policy Engine | Complete | 7-rule explore/exploit/escalate decision tree |
| 5 | Temporal Trending + Gap Detection | Complete | Context drift, silent failure, trend labels |
| 6 | Trust + Suspension Lifecycle | Complete | Exponential smoothing, 4 trust statuses |
| 7 | Sequence Learning (IPS) | Complete | Counterfactual tracking with IPS weights |
| 8 | 3-Tier Simulation Engine | Complete | Wilson CI → LightGBM → MCTS |
| 9 | Training + World Model | Complete | LightGBM quantile regression, cron retraining |
| 10 | Dashboard + Onboarding | Complete | React + Vite, 8+ pages |

### 3.2 What Actually Exists (Code-Audited)

| Component | Status | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| **Database schema** | ✅ Complete | 133 SQL files (127 migrations 001–131), 22+ tables, materialized views, RLS |
| **REST API** | ✅ Complete | 13 routes, 20+ lib modules, 5 middleware, 457-line entry point |
| **Dashboard** | ✅ Complete | 8+ pages (Overview, Onboarding, Outcomes, Simulate, Trust, Terms, Privacy, Settings) |
| **Edge Functions** | ✅ Complete | 6 Deno functions deployed on Supabase |
| **ML Training Pipeline** | ✅ Complete | LightGBM quantile regression, validation, export |
| **MCP Server** | ✅ Built | `@layerinfinite/mcp-server` v1.0.0 — 10 tools, pre-compiled to dist/, tested |
| **SDK Instrumentation (TS)** | ⚠️ Partially tested | v0.3.0 with tracing/interceptors — limited test coverage |
| **SDK Instrumentation (Python)** | ⚠️ Partially tested | v0.1.0 with tracing — limited test coverage |
| **Trading Connector** | ❌ Not built | Full plan exists (research.md) but no code |
| **CI/CD** | ⚠️ Partial | 4 workflows: CI, prod-readiness checks, npm/pypi publish |
| **Load testing** | ❌ None | No benchmark methodology or results |
| **Production deployment** | ❌ None | No evidence of production traffic or SLOs |

### 3.3 Architecture Diagram (As-Built)

```
┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│     AI AGENTS (Claude/Cursor/Cline) ↔ MCP Server ↔ REST API     │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                                  │ HTTP
┌─────────────────────────────────▼─────────────────────────────────┐
│                   API RUNTIME (Hono + TypeScript)                  │
│                                                                   │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  MIDDLEWARE STACK                                          │    │
│  │  ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────┐ ┌──────────────┐ │    │
│  │  │ Auth     │ │ Rate     │ │ Validate │ │ Sanitize     │ │    │
│  │  │ (API Key)│ │ Limit    │ │ Action   │ │ (PII, proto) │ │    │
│  │  └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────────┘ │    │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                   │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  CORE LIBRARIES                                            │    │
│  │                                                             │    │
│  │  scoring.ts         ← 5-factor composite score formula     │    │
│  │  policy-engine.ts   ← 7-rule decision tree (pure function) │    │
│  │  context-embed.ts   ← pgvector embedding + similarity      │    │
│  │  decision-writer.ts ← immutable decision record creation   │    │
│  │  schema-inferrer.ts ← action schema inference              │    │
│  │  ingest-core.ts     ← outcome ingestion pipeline           │    │
│  │                                                             │    │
│  │  outcome-orchestrator.ts ← 8 parallel post-outcome tasks   │    │
│  │  outcome-ingest-queue.ts ← durable outcome queue           │    │
│  │  outcome-score-inference.ts ← score inference from context │    │
│  │                                                             │    │

### 4.1 Migration Inventory

**All migrations** in `layer5/supabase/migrations/`:
- **127 SQL files** numbered 001–131
- **Gaps:** 030, 068, 069, 070 (intentionally skipped)
- **No separate api/migrations/** — all migrations consolidated in supabase/

**Non-migration SQL files (6):**
- `supabase/seed/cold_start_priors.sql` — seed data
- `scripts/verify-pgcron.sql` — cron verification
- `scripts/run-backfill-104.sql` — data backfill
- `tests/layer1/test_gate.sql`–`layer3/test_gate.sql` — test gates

### 4.2 Migration Ranges

| Range | Files | Focus | Key Objects |
|-------|-------|-------|-------------|
| 001–029 | 29 | Core schema | Dimensions, fact_outcomes, episodes, MVs, RLS, trust, events, indexes, cron, vector, auth, scoring, decisions, sequences, counterfactuals, world_model, notification, idempotency |
| 031–067 | 37 | Enhancements + Fixes | Verifier, validation mode, sandbox, backprop, rate limiting, backfills, object fixes, matview cron, data integrity, seed removal, trust snapshots, embedding versioning, drift, mv episode patterns rewrite, trust audit fix, mv confidence floor, refresh scores RPC |
| 071–131 | 61 | Maturity + Stability | Task grouping, pipeline hardening, context vector fixes, 500 error fixes, institutional knowledge seed, signal contracts, discrepancy log, tenant merge, mv tap fixes, unique constraints, customer_id scoping, sequence tenant invariants, cohort cycles, ingestion quality, scoring tiers, cross-event taxonomy, task performance MV, import jobs, inference, action aliases, backfills, clustering, step performance, context-aware MVs, dashboard indexes, ingress queue, retention policy, waitlist, debug, pgcrypto fix, trigger path fix |

### 4.3 Core Schema

**dim_customers**
- `customer_id UUID PK`, `company_name`, `industry`, `tier`, `api_key_hash`, `pruning_config JSONB`, `config JSONB`
- RLS: service_role only

**dim_agents**
- `agent_id UUID PK`, `customer_id FK`, `agent_type`, `llm_model`, `tools_available TEXT[]`
- RLS: scoped by customer

**fact_outcomes** (the central table — append-only)
- `outcome_id UUID PK`, `customer_id FK`, `agent_id FK`, `action_id FK`, `context_id FK`
- `success BOOLEAN`, `outcome_score NUMERIC`, `composite_score NUMERIC`
- `confidence NUMERIC`, `trend_delta NUMERIC`, `raw_context TEXT`
- `episode_id UUID`, `decision_id UUID`, `idempotency_key TEXT UNIQUE`
- `timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ`, `ingestion_source TEXT`, `task TEXT`, `resource_cost NUMERIC`
- **BEFORE UPDATE trigger raises EXCEPTION** — truly append-only
- Indexes: (customer_id, context_id, timestamp DESC), (agent_id, timestamp DESC), (customer_id, task, agent_id, source)

**mv_action_scores** (materialized view, refreshed ~5 min)
- Pre-aggregates per (customer_id, context_id, action_id): `weighted_success_rate`, `confidence`, `trend_delta`, `total_attempts`, `avg_response_ms`, `last_outcome_at`, `view_refreshed_at`

**agent_trust_scores**
- `agent_id PK`, `trust_score NUMERIC`, `trust_status TEXT` (trusted/probation/sandbox/suspended/new/degraded)
- `consecutive_failures INT`, `total_decisions INT`, `correct_decisions INT`

### 4.4 Trust Score Lifecycle

```
Cold start → trust_score = 0.70 (initial prior)
    │
    ├── On failure: score × 0.9^n (n = consecutive failures)
    │   └── 5 failures → 'degraded'
    │   └── 10 failures → 'suspended'
    │
    ├── On success: score × 1.03
    │   └── score ≥ 0.6 → 'trusted'
    │   └── score 0.3–0.6 → 'probation'
    │
    └── Coordinated failure detection: 3+ agents failing same action
        in 5 minutes → freeze trust (infrastructure attribution)
```

### 4.5 Key Constraints & Invariants

- `fact_outcomes`: UPDATE trigger raises EXCEPTION (append-only enforcement)
- `schema_migrations`: version tracked, rollback supported
- `fact_outcomes`: idempotency_key unique constraint prevents duplicates
- Rate limit: DB-backed via `consume_rate_limit_bucket` RPC with fail-open behavior (SECURITY ISSUE)
- RLS on all tenant-scoped tables (though some have `authenticated = TRUE` — SECURITY ISSUE)

### 5.4 The Simulation Engine (3-Tier)

| Tier | File | Technique | Latency | Purpose |
|------|------|-----------|---------|---------|
| Tier 1 | `tier1.ts` | Wilson Score Interval + bootstrap | <10ms | Fast estimate for <5 samples per action |
| Tier 2 | `tier2.ts` | LightGBM quantile regression (q10, q50, q90) | <100ms | Learned outcome prediction for 5–200 samples |
| Tier 3 | `tier3-mcts.ts` | MCTS with UCT: 500 sims, MAX_DEPTH=5, 8s timeout | ~7,500ms | Deep search for >200 samples or high-stakes |

**World Model** (`world-model.ts`):
- LightGBM tree evaluation: 600 evaluations per prediction (200 trees × 3 quantiles)
- 30-min cache per (context_hash, action_hash)
- Canary routing with configurable traffic percentage (`canary_traffic_pct`)
- 4 validation gates before model activation (R² ≥ 0.20, RMSE ≤ 0.35, CI coverage ≥ 85%, interval width ≤ 0.60)

### 5.5 The Scoring Formula

```
composite_score = (
    0.40 × success_rate   +  // Bayesian smoothed: (successes + 1) / (total + 2)
    0.20 × confidence     +  // Wilson-style: n/(n+10)
    0.20 × trend          +  // Normalized trend_delta to [0,1]
    0.10 × salience       +  // Action importance (currently always 1.0)
    0.10 × recency           // Decay over 7 days: max(0, 1 - age_hours/168)
) × context_match            // Cosine similarity to context vector
```

**Cold Start Fallback (3 tiers):**
1. Customer-specific `mv_action_scores` data
2. Context-specific `dim_institutional_knowledge` priors
3. Global fallback `dim_institutional_knowledge WHERE context_type = 'global'`

### 5.6 Policy Engine Decision Tree

```
1. Suspended agent            → ESCALATE
2. New agent (no history)     → EXPLORE (lowest-sample action)
3. Degraded agent             → SANDBOX (human review required)
4. Sandbox agent              → SANDBOX
5. Cold start / low confidence → EXPLORE (lowest-sample action)
6. Conservative + score > 0.8 → EXPLOIT
7. High score + high confidence → epsilon-greedy
8. Medium score (0.5–0.85)    → confidence-weighted exploit/explore
9. All scores < escalation    → ESCALATE
10. Default                   → EXPLORE (2nd action)
```

### 5.7 Most Critical Route: `POST /v1/log-outcome`

This is the most complex route in the system (1,603 lines — the single largest file).

**Middleware chain:** `primaryAuth` → `rateLimitMiddleware` → `validateActionMiddleware`

**Handler pipeline:**
1. Zod validation (30+ fields)
2. PII scrubbing via `sanitize.ts`
3. Task inference (`inferTask()` — maps issue_type to canonical task name)
4. Score inference (if `outcome_score` absent)
5. Decision linkage (lookup `fact_decisions` by `decision_id`)
6. Data quality computation
7. Semantic cluster inference
8. Durable queue enqueue (in production) or direct insert

**Post-outcome orchestration** (via `Promise.allSettled`):
- Trust update (exponential smoothing + coordinated failure check)
- Context drift detection
- Silent failure detection
- Counterfactual writing
- Sequence tracking + reward backpropagation
- Latency spike detection
- Score cache invalidation
- Predictive drift analysis

## 8. Machine Learning Training Pipeline

**Location:** `layer5/training/`

**Pipeline:**
```
features.py → train_world_model.py → validate_model.py → export_model.py
                  ↓
         world_model_artifacts (DB table)
                  ↓
         tier-selector.ts → tier2.ts (inference)
```

**Model:** LightGBM quantile regression (3 quantiles: q10, q50, q90)
- 200 trees per quantile model
- 10 input features: action_id, episode_position, prev_actions ×3, context_frequency, time features (hour, day, month, weekday)
- Training frequency: weekly cron (via Cloud Run Job)

**Validation Gates** (all must pass for model activation):
- R² ≥ 0.20 (explains at least 20% of outcome variance)
- RMSE ≤ 0.35 (on 0–1 scale)
- 95% CI coverage ≥ 0.85
- Average interval width ≤ 0.60

**Dockerfile + cloudrun-job.yaml:** Deployed to Google Cloud Run as a batch job.

### 11.1 Architecture

```
AI Agent (Claude/Cursor/Cline)
  │ stdio transport (stdin/stdout)
  ▼
packages/mcp-server/
  ├── bin/cli.ts           ← StdioServerTransport entry point
  ├── src/index.ts         ← createServer() factory, registers tools/resources/prompts
  ├── src/tools/           ← 10 tool implementations
  ├── src/resources/       ← task-intelligence resource
  ├── src/prompts/         ← agent-setup prompt
  ├── src/rest-client.ts   ← HTTP client to LI REST API
  ├── src/episode-tracker.ts ← Redirect loop prevention (30-min expiry)
  ├── src/param-resolver.ts← 3-layer parameter resolution
  ├── src/config.ts        ← Env validation + config freeze
  └── src/logger.ts        ← Structured JSON logger (stderr)
  │
  ▼ HTTP
LI REST API (layer5/api/)
```

### 11.2 10 Tools — Mode-Gated Availability

| Tool | Bootstrap | Recommend | Assist | Auto |
|------|-----------|-----------|--------|------|
| `li_log` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_observe` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_audit` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_health` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_toggle_action` | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_simulate` | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_patterns` | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_action` | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_fallback` | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ | ✅ |
| `li_register_action` | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ (admin only) |

### 11.3 4 Modes

Controlled by `LAYERINFINITE_MODE` env var:
- **(absent) = Bootstrap** — log/observe only. LI learns from outcomes.
- **`recommend`** — LI injects scored recommendations via `layerinfinite://tasks/{task}` resource.
- **`assist`** — `li_action` warns if a better action exists; agent decides.
- **`auto`** — `li_action` redirects to proven best action (needs ≥90% confidence). Falls back to assist.

### 11.4 Key Implementation Details

- **RestClient:** Exponential backoff retries, timeout handling, structured error responses
- **EpisodeTracker:** In-memory Set with 30-min TTL expiry — prevents redirect loops
- **ParamResolver:** 3-layer resolution (carry-over → defaults → required params) for auto-mode redirects
- **Logger:** Structured JSON to stderr with levels and tool scoping
- **Config:** Frozen config object after env validation — fails fast on missing API key
- **Graceful shutdown:** SIGINT/SIGTERM handling with debounce
- **Pre-compiled:** `dist/` directory ready to run — no build step needed

- `config.test.ts` — env validation, mode parsing, config freeze
- `episode-tracker.test.ts` — mark, check, expiry, idempotency
- `li-action.test.ts` — 9 integration tests (cold start, scores, assist warn, auto redirect, loop prevention, downgrade)
- `param-resolver.test.ts` — 3-layer resolution, missing params, optional params
